mnemonics: ROCKET SHIP: ROC curves evaluate models, SHIP stands for Sensitivity (Recall), Hit rate (TPR), 1-Specificity (FPR).
keyPoints: AUC-ROC measures the model's ability to distinguish between classes, not its absolute probability calibration.
Precision-Recall curves are often more informative than ROC curves for highly imbalanced datasets.
The choice between optimizing for Precision or Recall is a business decision based on the cost of false positives vs. false negatives.
commonMistakes: Using accuracy as the sole metric for a highly imbalanced classification problem (e.g., where 95% are 'No').
Assuming a high AUC (e.g., > 0.9) always means the model has good predictive performance, without checking the PR curve.
